launch_cutline.description: A launch-blocking requirement backs a Stable (or LTS) public claim only when its proof packet is within its freshness SLO, any waiver it relies on is unexpired, its requirement-level evidence is complete, an owner has signed off, and the public claim it backs is itself at or above the cutline. A requirement that loses any of those backs a label below the cutline (beta, preview, withdrawn) and never inherits an adjacent requirement's proven label.

proof_rules.title: Backing public claim narrowed below cutline
proof_rules.rationale: A requirement whose backing public claim is already below the stable cutline inherits that ceiling; the stable claim manifest already holds the claim upstream, so this is expected inheritance rather than a proof-index defect and does not hold publication on its own.

proof_rules.title: Requirement names an unimplemented capability
proof_rules.rationale: A launch-blocking requirement that names a capability the build does not yet implement cannot back a Stable public claim; the proof narrows and publication holds until the capability lands and is re-proven.

proof_rules.title: Requirement-level evidence incomplete
proof_rules.rationale: A requirement whose proof packet does not cover the requirement-level evidence narrows until the evidence is recaptured; the public claim cannot publish Stable on partial proof.

proof_rules.title: Proof packet breached its freshness SLO
proof_rules.rationale: A requirement cannot back a Stable public claim on a proof packet that has aged past its freshness SLO; the proof narrows and the packet must be refreshed before it backs the claim again.

proof_rules.title: No proof packet captured
proof_rules.rationale: A launch-blocking requirement with no captured proof packet has nothing to prove against and narrows until a packet lands.

proof_rules.title: Provisional waiver expired
proof_rules.rationale: A requirement that only backed a Stable claim because of a waiver narrows the moment the waiver expires; publication holds rather than silently keeping the proof.

proof_rules.title: Proven requirement lacks owner sign-off
proof_rules.rationale: No requirement backs a Stable or LTS public claim without a named owner sign-off; an unsigned proof holds publication until the owner signs.

rows.title: Provider routing and fallback is proven
rows.requirement_summary: Provider-aware routing selects a healthy provider and falls back deterministically when the primary is unavailable.
rows.rationale: The provider routing/fallback proof packet is current within its freshness SLO, the owner has signed off, and the public claim it backs publishes Stable, so this launch-blocking requirement is proven at the Stable label.

rows.title: Provider completion quality is unproven (stale packet)
rows.requirement_summary: Provider-aware completions meet the published acceptance and latency bar across the reference corpus.
rows.rationale: The completion-quality proof packet aged past its freshness SLO, so the packet-freshness automation narrows this launch-blocking requirement to beta even though the public claim it backs is still published Stable; the proof must be refreshed before the claim can rest on it again.

rows.title: Rollback live-hardware drill is proven on waiver
rows.requirement_summary: A live-hardware rollback drill restores the prior version and state without data loss within the published window.

waiver.reason: The rollback contract and dry-run evidence are current; an active waiver covers the still-pending live-hardware drill while it is scheduled.

rows.rationale: The rollback drill requirement backs the Stable claim under an active, unexpired waiver covering the scheduled live-hardware drill; its proof packet is within the SLO but inside the warn window (due for refresh), and the waiver and its expiry are recorded so the proof narrows automatically if it lapses.

rows.title: Rollback state integrity is unproven (waiver expired)
rows.requirement_summary: On-disk state survives a rollback round trip with no schema corruption or silent loss.

waiver.reason: The waiver that let rollback state integrity back the Stable claim while the corruption-soak evidence was gathered has expired without that evidence landing.

rows.rationale: The provisional waiver expired before the corruption-soak evidence landed, so this launch-blocking requirement narrows to beta even though its proof packet is current and the public claim it backs is still published Stable; publication holds until the evidence lands.

rows.title: Export/offboarding fidelity inherits a narrowed claim
rows.requirement_summary: A full export round-trips into a clean install without losing project state, history, or settings.
rows.rationale: The export/offboarding proof packet is current, but the public claim it backs is itself narrowed to beta upstream, so the proof inherits that ceiling and backs beta. This is expected inheritance from the stable claim manifest, not a proof-index defect, so it does not hold publication on its own.

rows.title: Localization locale coverage is unbacked under a narrowed claim
rows.requirement_summary: Every shipped locale renders the launch surfaces without truncation, fallback gaps, or untranslated strings.
rows.rationale: Locale coverage names per-locale rendering the pre-implementation build does not yet provide, and the public claim it backs is already narrowed to preview, so the proof backs preview. The capability gap is recorded, but because the claim is below the cutline it does not hold publication beyond the upstream narrowing.

rows.title: Regulated sovereign-deploy evidence has no captured packet
rows.requirement_summary: A sovereign / air-gapped deployment passes the regulated-environment evidence run end to end.
rows.rationale: No sovereign-deploy evidence run has been captured, so the requirement has no proof packet at all; the public claim it backs is already narrowed to beta, so the proof withdraws its stable assertion entirely. The missing packet is recorded but does not hold publication beyond the upstream narrowing.

rows.title: Regulated control-plane attestation is unsigned (advisory requirement)
rows.requirement_summary: The managed control-plane attestation packet is signed by the regulated-governance owner.
rows.rationale: The control-plane attestation packet is captured but the requirement-level evidence is incomplete and the regulated-governance owner has not signed it; this is an advisory (non-launch-blocking) requirement and the public claim it backs is already narrowed to beta, so the proof backs beta and does not hold publication.

publication.rationale: Two launch-blocking requirements that back public claims still published Stable are themselves unproven: provider completion quality rides a proof packet past its freshness SLO, and rollback state integrity lost its provisional waiver. Their reasons fire two blocking proof rules, so stable proof-index publication holds until the completion-quality packet is refreshed and the rollback evidence lands or those public claims are formally narrowed.
